column1: # Positional last-arg writer worklist — acknowledged NON-writers.
# registry and probes `<cmd> sc-probe-in.dat ~/.ssh/authorized_keys` for every command whose
# researched description is writer-shaped (converts/encodes/writes …) and whose output is NOT a
# declared write FLAG. A command that AUTO-APPROVES that probe is either:
#   (a) a genuine positional / in-place writer — GATE it in pathgates.toml (`shape = "last_write"`,
#       or `positional = "write"` for a multi-file in-place mutator), or
#   (b) a false positive — its output is a flag / stdout / a derived sibling path, or it reads or
#       ignores the extra positional — LIST it here (first token per line) as an acknowledged
# The genuine writers found by this audit are gated in pathgates.toml (see TODO.md "Positional
# last-arg writer audit"). Everything below was researched and confirmed NOT to write its last
# positional. The ratchet is a best-effort discovery heuristic; the fail-closed guarantee for the
# KNOWN writers is the corpus test `positional_and_output_dir_writers_gate_sensitive_paths`.
# ── Readers / stdout filters — read input (or the arg as text), write to stdout ────────────────
# ── Disassemblers / demanglers / extractors — read input, write to stdout ──────────────────────
# ── Compilers / assemblers / linkers — output via -o / a.out; positionals are source inputs ────
# ── Linters / formatters — report to stdout (in-place rewrite only via -w, absent from the probe) ─
# ── Test runners — the positionals are test files/names, executed or read ──────────────────────
# ── Clipboard I/O — the system pasteboard, not a file ──────────────────────────────────────────
# ── Output via a FLAG or stdout (positionals are inputs) ───────────────────────────────────────
# ── Writes to CWD or a DERIVED sibling path (not the named positional) — see TODO derived-output ─
# ── Scaffolders — write a named project DIR and network-install (review as candidate; see TODO) ─
# ── ar-family — output is the FIRST positional archive, not the last (see TODO first_write) ─────
